title: Typed Tool Contracts, JSON Schema, Descriptions, Domains, and Versions
description: A broad execute tool accepts free-form strings, hides side effects, uses ambiguous parameter names, has no bounds or schema version, and exposes more authority than the task needs. Learners cumulatively produce least-capability typed tool catalog through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define one narrow tool purpose, effect class, caller, user benefit, authority, timeout, idempotency, and prohibited use.
Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.
Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.
